objective: Bootstrap the narrow parent-owned #612 hygiene child that reconciles closed child #629 out of active OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md state while preserving completed-history visibility.
scope_boundary: Planning-only hygiene child lane under issue #612.
Owns OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md reconciliation for closed child #629 only, plus issue-local artifacts.
Must preserve completed-history visibility for #629 while removing its active In Progress representation.
Must not widen into #632 implementation surfaces, #629 implementation surfaces, broader #612 enforcement code, or any other governance mirrors unless matching stale #629 drift is proven first.
out_of_scope: Reopening issue #629 fallback-log schema or workflow implementation.
Broader #612 validator, workflow, or routing enforcement.
Edits to docs/PROGRESS.md, docs/FAIL_FAST_LOG.md, or docs/FEATURE_REGISTRY.md unless matching stale #629 mirror state is concretely proven.
research_summary: Issue #632 local-ci replay exposed that OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md still lists closed child #629 as active work, causing repo-level overlord-backlog-alignment failure even though #632's owned implementation gap is closed. Specialist review confirmed the narrowest valid owner is a new parent-owned #612 hygiene child limited to OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md reconciliation for #629 only.

sprints.name: Packet Bootstrap
sprints.goal: Create the issue-local planning packet and keep #633 bounded to the single stale #629 backlog mirror row.
tasks: Create the issue-local PDCAR, structured plan, planning execution scope, handoff, review packet, cross-review note, validation artifact, and review placeholder.
Record the exact stale #629 row and the requirement to preserve completed-history visibility while removing active state.
Keep the lane in planning_only mode until the packet validates and alternate-family review is recorded.
acceptance_criteria: The packet names OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md and issue-local artifacts as the only owned surfaces.
The packet states that #629 must leave In Progress and remain visible in Done history.
The packet preserves #612, #629, and #632 substantive implementation surfaces as external boundaries.
The execution scope allows only issue-local planning artifacts during bootstrap.
Implementation remains blocked until planning validation and alternate-family review complete.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify whether the stale closed-#629 mirror row belongs to #632 or requires a new parent-owned #612 hygiene child.
specialist_reviews.summary: The stale #629 row is real on current origin/main and belongs to a new parent-owned #612 hygiene child limited to OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md only.

specialist_reviews.focus: Verify the lane stays limited to stale #629 mirror hygiene and does not reopen broader #612 work.
specialist_reviews.summary: The acceptable boundary is OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md plus issue-local artifacts only, preserving Done visibility for #629 and excluding all broader #612, #629, and #632 implementation surfaces.

alternate_model_review.summary: Sanctioned alternate-family review passed for the planning-only #633 packet with only cosmetic follow-up: specialist status labels should read pass, and the validation artifact should reconcile the real review transport/evidence chain.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #633 is authorized for the bounded OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md hygiene slice only: reconcile closed child #629 out of active In Progress state while preserving completed-history visibility, plus issue-local artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement the single-row OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md reconciliation for closed child #629, prove backlog-alignment passes, and close the slice without widening into broader #612, #629, or #632 surfaces.
blocked_on: Issue #612 remains the parent fallback-enforcement lane and is not closed by #633 alone.
Issue #632 remains a bounded implementation child and must not absorb the parent-owned backlog mirror fix.
handoff_acceptance_criteria: AC1: Issue #633 remains bounded to OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md closed-child #629 mirror hygiene and issue-local artifacts only.
AC2: OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md no longer lists closed issue #629 as active In Progress work and preserves completed-history visibility in Done.
AC3: check_overlord_backlog_github_alignment.py no longer fails on the closed-#629 active-row defect.
AC4: Issue #633 preserves #612, #629, and #632 substantive implementation surfaces as external boundaries and does not claim broader #612 closure.

material_deviation_rules: Any edit outside OVERLORD_BACKLOG.md and issue-local artifacts is a material deviation unless matching stale #629 mirror drift is first proven and the scope is widened explicitly.
Any edit to #629 implementation surfaces is a material deviation.
Any edit to #632 implementation surfaces is a material deviation.
Any claim that #633 closes broader #612 enforcement is a material deviation.
